code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1★ (Very Negative):
    - Multi-purchase failures with safety hazards/defects
    - Core functionality failure despite proper usage
    - Negative updates invalidating previous satisfaction
    - Explicit "avoid purchase" warnings with evidence

    2★ (Negative):
    - Requires persistent workarounds for basic function
    - Clear inferiority to alternatives in critical aspects
    - Partial functionality with recurring frustration
    - Regret statements with cost-benefit justification

    3★ (Neutral/Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with contextual limitations
    - Adequate performance with notable compromises
    - Target-specific suitability without recommendation
    - Conditional praise ("works if...") lacking endorsement

    4★ (Positive):
    - Single functional flaw impacting core utility
    - Explicit star deduction ("only 4 because...")
    - Requires break-in period for optimal performance
    - Assembly/setup challenges despite final satisfaction

    5★ (Very Positive):
    - Excels in 3+ aspects (functionality/durability/design)
    - Solves persistent problems exceeding expectations
    - Close brand comparisons with satisfaction (+minor differences)
    - Non-functional criticism WITH enthusiastic core praise
    - Concise praise without negatives ("Fits perfectly")
    - Explicit "worth it" statements despite minor flaws
    - Long-term validation with sustained performance

    BOUNDARY CLARIFICATIONS:
    - Functional praise + usage limitation →4 (e.g. capacity)
    - Praise + non-functional criticism →5 (e.g. color)
    - Multi-purchase failures →1 regardless of initial success
    - "Nearly matches X" comparisons →5 if user satisfied
    - Minor setup/learning curve →5 if core function flawless
    - Explicit "still 5 stars" overrides non-functional flaws
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example1:
Review: "Second unit failed faster. Dangerous defects!"
<Reasoning>Multi-purchase safety failures override positives →1</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example2:
Review: "Does exactly what needed - deep pockets fit any mattress"
<Reasoning>Concise functional praise without negatives →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example3:
Review: "Not quite Lodge but very close! Daily driver despite minor differences"
<Reasoning>Close brand comparison with satisfaction →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example4:
Review: "These towels need 2 washes for absorbency but work well after"
<Reasoning>Functional prerequisite impacts experience →4</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example5:
Review: "Sharp tablecloth. Only con is needing to iron cotton"
<Reasoning>Non-functional maintenance vs core excellence →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example6:
Review: "First lasted 3 years, second failed in 2 months. Never again"
<Reasoning>Multi-purchase defects override initial success →1</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example7:
Review: "Steam buttons confuse initially but clean flawlessly once learned"
<Reasoning>Minor learning curve with core excellence →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example8:
Review: "Good for light use. Thin material concerns for heavy loads"
<Reasoning>Adequate with notable compromise →3</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example9:
Review: "Constantly retighten screws - not worth the effort"
<Reasoning>Excessive maintenance →2</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example10:
Review: "Edges stay stiff after washing but overall effective"
<Reasoning>Lingering functional drawback →4</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
Rating:
</Task>
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (25 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 14
  gold 4 -> 3: 3
  gold 5 -> 4: 3
  gold 1 -> 2: 2
  gold 1 -> 3: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "It's a very solid bed that feels like it's going to hold up to just about anything. Assembly wasn't too difficult, though there were a couple of parts that had to be under some pressure to get the screw holes to line up just right. Also, when we firs..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [4, 3, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "This looks like a great idea and easy to use, but actually it's very awkward. The idea is to hang your jewelry in the closet. But the hanger part doesn't swivel, so if you want to select an item, you have to remove it from the closet and lay it on th..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 2 | Workers: [1, 2, 2] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "I'm partial to the Presto Pop Lite hot air popper, but it looks cheap and plasticy, so I tried this.

All in all this works like the Presto or any other hot air popper, but it has a couple nuances (good and bad):

This unit is more compact and attrac..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 2, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "On my computer screen the color for the 'cool brown' bath mat is accurate.  It's a faded sort of brown with lots of gray tint to it.  Our upstairs bath is in browns -- golden-hue brown and tile brown-- and it fits in.

What you get is a cool-brown no..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "The color pattern here is very pleasant. The rug looks great. I wish I had tiled my whole floor like this. The non-stick feature is sprayed on rather that woven into the rug. I hope that it holds up."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "I like my Star Wars characters. I have a Darth Vader mug already and now I have the Chewbacca. The cup is nice and I like that it has the sash detail on the front and back so you know immediately it is Chewie. The mug can hold 20 ounces. As with most..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "I can forgive the fact that there was blood on one of the math mats when I opened the package.  Things happen and I'm sorry that someone was injured.  What I can't get past is the fact that there are so many snags.  It's like these two mats were seco..."
     Gold: 2 | Predicted: 2 | Workers: [2, 4, 2] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "This is a good, though not extra ordinary storage bin. It lacks exceptional build quality, exceptional attention to detail, or anything that's all that remarkable. It looks good, and the pattern is timeless, plus it keeps stuff together. You could do..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "EDIT: A year of use later it's still humming along and doing its job just fine. Sometimes it seems like it doesn't pick up quickly that it should be upping its speed on its own in the auto setting, but maybe it's just taking longer for the air to rea..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 25 errors, 20 borderline out of 80 examples."}
